book.descrip.hexflow:pure_map: Similar to $(l:patterns/meta#hexcasting:for_each)$(action)Thoth's Gambit/$, but won't copy the original stack inside each sub-execution.
book.descrip.hexflow:pure_reduce: Similar to $(l:patterns/meta#hexcasting:for_each)$(action)Thoth's Gambit/$; for data list with length $(bold)n/$, it executes $(bold)n - 1/$ times; The first initial stack is the first two elements, and each subsequent execution uses the stack after the last execution.
book.descrip.hexflow:for_range/cube: Executes given list on each point as initial iota inside the cuboid defined by given 2 points as body diagonal.
book.descrip.hexflow:for_range/cube.2: Optional number (valid range: $(bold)0 - 3/$) stands for their iteration order: $(bold)0/$ (by default) for no sorting, $(bold)1/$ for nearest first, $(bold)2/$ for farthest first, and $(bold)3/$ for randomly shuffled.
book.descrip.hexflow:for_range/floodfill: Executes given list on each point as initial iota which is connected with given points with the same block type, and within casting ambit.

First optional number ($(bold)1/$ by default, valid range: $(bold)1 - 7/$) is used for configuring spreading ways bitwise ($(bold)1/$ = 6 faces, $(bold)2/$ = 12 edges, $(bold)4/$ = 8 corners), and second one (a positive integer) for limiting max chain positions count.
book.descrip.hexflow:for_range/?/pure: ...and yes, there are pure version for those shaped mapping patterns, too.$(br)Everything the same, except that original stack isn't copied into each sub-executions.
book.descrip.hexflow:for_range/?/pure.2: Everything the same, except that original stack isn't copied into each sub-executions.
book.descrip.hexflow:build_nested: Copy list #0 and set back to index #1
book.descrip.hexflow:nested_modify: List iota #0 is the nested list to be modified. According to the index sequence given by list #1, traverses to the inner layer, and sets the corresponding element to iota #2.
book.descrip.hexflow:noob_num: The starting cumulative number is 0; putting the initial direction to →, all ↗ makes the result $(bold)* 2 + 1/$, all → makes it $(bold)* 2/$, and all ↘ for $(bold)/ 10/$. Starting with the patterns on the right negates the result.
book.descrip.hexflow:mass_rotate: Accepts the size to reorder and a bottom-to-top order list, and do the corresponding reorder to the rest of the stack. For example, $(bold)3/$ plus $(bold)[1,2,0]/$ for $(l:patterns/stackmanip#hexcasting:rotate)$(action)Rotation Gambit/$, and $(bold)2/$ plus $(bold)[0,1,0,1]/$ for $(l:patterns/stackmanip#hexcasting:2dup)$(action)Dioscuri Gambit/$
book.descrip.hexflow:weak_escape: Same as $(l:patterns/patterns_as_iotas#hexcasting:escape)$(action)Consideration/$, but with lowest execution priority, and won't grow in $(bold)2^n/$ formula exponentially inside nested $(l:patterns/patterns_as_iotas#hexcasting:open_paren)$(action)Intro-Retro/$s
book.descrip.hexflow:copy_mask: Each "n" on the fisherman pattern's bottom fishing line makes a copy of correspond iota to stack top, following the same targeting rules as $(l:patterns/stackmanip#hexcasting:mask)$(action)Bookkeeper's Gambit/$.
book.descrip.hexflow:call_stack: Executes given pattern/list/Jump? iota in an independent stack, then recovers the original stack at bottom. If given an integer argument X no more than stack size, first X iotas will be taken away as the initial new stack.
